summary:
Phase C v2 enhancements (bulk connect + device profiles + per-device primary source overrides) tested on the public preview URL. Backend: 21/22 pytest cases pass (test_healthbridge_v6_phase_c2.py). The one failure is a NEW regression on GET /api/metrics/summary returning HTTP 500 due to a Pydantic ResponseValidationError — the response model still uses a Literal of 8 metric names while DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE has expanded to 32+ metrics. Bulk connect with platforms=['ios'] correctly connects exactly the 7 iOS-compatible connectors (apple_health, fitbit, garmin, myfitnesspal, strava, oura, withings) and leaves google_fit/samsung_health disconnected. platforms=['android'] mirrors that for 8 android-compatible. {} connects all 9. connector_ids=['fitbit','garmin'] connects only those two. Device-profile register+list works (upsert verified). /metrics/availability now echoes `device_id` at the top level and includes `primary_is_device_specific` on every metric — False when no device_id is provided, True when a per-device override exists. POST /connectors/primary with device_id scopes the override to that device only (global stays untouched); DELETE /connectors/primary/{metric}?device_id=... removes ONLY the device override and falls back to global. Disconnecting a connector that was both global AND device-specific primary correctly reassigns BOTH entries to another connected provider (fitbit). 400/404 paths preserved.

Frontend e2e on 390x844: /app-connectors (fresh user) shows `connectors-summary` banner + `bulk-connect-btn` reading 'Connect all 9 available apps'. Tap → SyncingOverlay → all 9 cards flip to 'Connected' badge and banner updates to '9 sources connected — metrics unlock automatically as data flows in.' ✓. /customize-metrics renders `primary-scope-card` with `primary-scope-device` (text 'This device') + `primary-scope-account`. With Device scope active, tapping `metric-primary-steps` (showing 'Apple Health') and selecting fitbit changes the chip to 'Fitbit' AND inserts a `metric-device-tag-steps` chip reading 'This device' with an X icon. Tapping the X (clearDeviceOverride) immediately reverts the chip to 'Apple Health' and removes the device tag ✓. Switching to All-devices scope and changing primary to Garmin updates the chip to 'Garmin Connect' with NO device tag (account-global write) ✓. ProximityScanModal on /(tabs)/watches: disconnecting the Apple Watch then tapping 'Connect Watch' opens `proximity-modal` → radar scans → shows 'Watch detected • RSSI -53 dBm • 2.3 m • IN RANGE' AND `proximity-source` testid shows 'SIMULATED SCAN (dev build only)' label, exactly per spec for the web fallback ✓.

critical.issue: Returns HTTP 500 — fastapi.exceptions.ResponseValidationError: 25 literal_error entries because the response model's `metric` field is Literal['steps','heart_rate','sleep','workouts','spo2','ecg','calories','stand'] (8 values) but DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE now emits 32+ metrics including 'distance', 'active_minutes', 'floors', 'workout_count', 'vo2_max', 'training_load', 'recovery_time', 'calorie_intake', 'protein', 'carbs', 'fat', 'water', 'fiber', 'weight', 'bmi', 'body_fat', 'muscle_mass', 'sleep_quality', 'resting_hr', 'hrv', 'blood_pressure_sys', 'blood_pressure_dia', 'respiratory_rate', 'body_temp', 'stress'. Either widen the Literal to MetricId, switch the model field to `str`, or drop response_model on this endpoint. Repro: GET /api/metrics/summary with admin bearer.

issues: Pre-existing warnings persist: props.pointerEvents is deprecated; 'shadow*' style props are deprecated; expo-notifications web limitation. Non-blocking.

action_items: FIX (HIGH) /api/metrics/summary 500 — the Pydantic Literal of 8 metric names no longer covers the 32+ metrics emitted by DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE. Either change the response model's `metric` field to `str` (or a shared MetricId enum), or remove `response_model=` on the route. The frontend dashboard relies on this endpoint; it will fail for any user who hits the Today's Progress / rings widget that depends on a summary fetch.

Recommend splitting server.py (now 2007 lines) into routers/ — connectors.py, devices.py, metrics.py, watches.py, ai_insights.py, billing.py, admin.py. The file has crossed 2x the 700-line threshold and Phase C v2 added another ~120 LOC.

Optional: surface the device label in the primary-scope-device button instead of the raw 'This device (This device)' fallback — useDevice() registered the device but the label appears to be the literal string 'This device' rather than the friendly device label. Verify whether useDevice() default-labels the device or pulls from a more specific source (navigator.userAgent on web).
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/metrics/summary regression is a strict-typing classic: a Literal field on a Pydantic response_model needs to be kept in sync with the data shape. Switching to `str` or a single MetricId TypeAlias used everywhere would prevent this class of bug recurring.

Connector ordering in bulk_connect (server.py:1350-1351) re-sorts using CONNECTOR_TEMPLATE order — good, matches list_connectors. Idempotency verified (calling connect-all twice returns the same shape, no duplicate primary inserts).

set_primary_source (server.py:1289) uses device_key = device_id or '*' for the upsert filter. metrics_availability (line 1232) reads `device_id: {$in: [None, '*']}` for globals — correct dual-key handling; legacy docs with device_id=null still resolve.

Disconnect re-assignment (server.py:1190-1209) iterates ALL primary docs (both '*' and device-specific) and reassigns each independently — verified by test_disconnect_reassigns_both_global_and_device.
DeviceRegisterIn enforces min_length=4 on device_id — good (prevents collisions on trivial values). Validated by test_register_validates_min_length (returns 422).
scanProximity web-fallback (services/healthBridge.ts) cleanly reports source='simulated' which the modal renders as 'SIMULATED SCAN (dev build only)' — discoverability is excellent.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_healthbridge_v6_phase_c2.py (created — 22 pytest cases for Phase C v2)

success_rate.backend: 95% (21/22 v6 Phase C2; the failing case is /metrics/summary 500 — pre-existing-style regression discovered in regression-smoke)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on all Phase C2 critical flows (bulk-connect-btn → 9 connected, primary-scope-card with device+account toggle, per-device override + device-tag clear, account-scope global write, proximity-source SIMULATED label)

test_credentials: admin@healthbridge.app / ySk4rWp4nSn5KsB8WvI4iF and demo@healthbridge.app / Demo1234! verified via /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Fresh per-test users use phc2_<uuid>@example.com / Pass1234! and clean up implicitly (orphan but unused).
seed_data_creation: Fresh users were created in tests via /api/auth/register; one frontend test user (phc2ui_<uuid>@example.com) is left with all 9 connectors connected and steps.primary set globally to garmin. Admin watches were toggled (one was disconnected then reconnected during proximity test — actually left in the post-modal-close state; main agent may want to reconnect via /api/watches/{id}/connect if a clean admin state is needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: BASE_URL=https://b5756d38-6d5c-4694-ae0a-e29e724ded9b.preview.emergentagent.com, API=/api. Phase C v2 endpoints all green except /metrics/summary which returns 500 due to Pydantic Literal mismatch. New testids verified: bulk-connect-btn, primary-scope-card, primary-scope-device, primary-scope-account, metric-device-tag-{metric}, proximity-source. localStorage tokens go under 'hbv_access_token' and 'hbv_refresh_token' as JSON-stringified strings — confirmed working seed pattern. To exercise per-device behaviour on web, useDevice() persists device_id at localStorage key '@healthbridge_device_id' — clearing it forces a new device profile.
rca_of_issue: /api/metrics/summary 500 — Root cause: the route's response_model has a Pydantic field declared `metric: Literal['steps','heart_rate','sleep','workouts','spo2','ecg','calories','stand']`. Since Phase C, DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE emits ~32 different metric ids (distance, floors, vo2_max, hrv, weight, etc.). FastAPI's serialize_response validates EACH item in the returned list against the Literal and raises ResponseValidationError when any value falls outside the 8 listed names. Mitigation options: (1) change the field to `metric: str`, (2) replace the Literal with a single shared MetricId enum/Literal sourced from DEFAULT_METRICS_TEMPLATE keys, (3) drop response_model on this endpoint and return raw dicts. Repro: curl with admin bearer GET /api/metrics/summary — see /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for 25-item validation error dump.
